senses.definition: colored again; changed the color of something previously colored
examples: She recolored the faded notebook with a fresh blue cover.
The artist recolored the painting to make the tones warmer.

senses.definition: having been recolored; colored again
examples: a recolored photograph that looks newer.
recolored artwork with brighter hues.

senses.definition: the act or process of coloring something again
examples: The recoloring of the fabric took longer than expected.
A slow recoloring of the picture was done to restore its tones.

senses.definition: to color something again; to change the color of something that has already been colored
examples: They are recoloring the mural to match the new design.
We recoloring the curtains to a warmer shade.

senses.definition: plural of recolor; instances or results of recoloring
examples: The recolors on the fabric show uneven shading.
The gallery displayed several recolors of the same painting.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of recolor; colors something again
examples: He recolors the diagram to improve contrast.
The software recolors pixels to reduce glare.

senses.definition: to change the color of something again (British spelling)
examples: They decided to recolour the walls to a lighter shade.
She recolours the map to show new territories.

senses.definition: the act or process of recolouring; the result of recolouring
examples: The recolouration of the logo altered brand perception.
A slow recolouration restored some original tones.

senses.definition: colored again; has had its color changed previously
examples: The recoloured photograph looked more vibrant.
He recoloured the fabric to hide stains.

senses.definition: having had its color changed again; color-changed
examples: recoloured pages in the album were inserted.
a recoloured version of the image

senses.definition: the act or process of recolouring; the result of recolouring
examples: The recolouring of the portrait took hours.
Recolouring can improve visibility in certain lighting.

senses.definition: plural of recolour; instances of recolouring
examples: The recolours of the poster were selective.
There are several recolours in this edition.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of recolour; changes color again (British spelling)
examples: It recolours the map to reflect new data.
The designer recolours the scheme for readability.

senses.definition: the act of recolouring; the result of recolouring
examples: The recolour of the painting altered its mood.
A quick recolour changed the overall look.

senses.definition: short form slang for recombinant or recombination; not a standard dictionary entry in formal use
examples: The scientist discussed a new recomb method.
They studied a bacterial recomb process.

senses.definition: combed again; ran a comb through again
examples: She recombed her hair to remove tangles.
He recombed the wig for a neater look.

senses.definition: an organism or molecule produced by recombination, often in genetics
examples: The study used a recombinant DNA technique.
Recombinant proteins are common in vaccines.

senses.definition: related to or produced by recombination
examples: recombinant technology
recombinant DNA methods

senses.definition: plural of recombinant; organisms or molecules produced by recombination
examples: Various recombinants were tested in the experiment.
The study compared different recombinants for efficiency.

senses.definition: the process by which genetic material is rearranged to produce new combinations
examples: DNA recombination is a key tool in genetics.
Recombination can create diversity in viruses.

senses.definition: relating to or caused by recombination
examples: recombinational strategies were used in the study.
recombinational genetics techniques.

senses.definition: plural of recombination; multiple events of genetic rearrangement
examples: Different recombinations were observed across samples.
The results showed several recombinations contributing to variation.

senses.definition: to combine genetic material in a new way; to recombine
examples: Researchers can recombine genes to study functions.
The virus can recombine with another strain.

senses.definition: past tense of recombine; combined genetic material differently
examples: The genome was recombined to express a new protein.
They recombined the sequences to test for function.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of recombine; combines material in a new way
examples: The virus recombines with another strain.
The software recombines data to create a new model.

senses.definition: present participle of recombe? wait; intended as recombe? actually recombing is nonstandard; treat as rare form of recombining
examples: They are recombing through the sequences to find overlaps.

senses.definition: present participle of recombine; engaging in recombination
examples: The researchers are recombining fragments to assemble the genome.
Recombining the segments can reveal new traits.

senses.definition: short for 'recombs' or a misspelling; not standard; likely intended as 'combs' with prefix re-
examples: He used recombs to style hair.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of recomb; see recombine
examples: He recombs the hair into waves.

senses.definition: nonstandard; not a recognized English word
examples: No standard meaning; may be a typo for 'remember' or 'remember'.

senses.definition: to comfort again; provide renewed comfort
examples: They tried to recomfort the anxious patient.
The team comforted and reassured her.

senses.definition: the act of comforting again; renewed comfort
examples: The comfort provided a sense of calm through discomfort.

senses.definition: past tense of recomfort; gave comfort again
examples: She recomforted the frightened child.
They recomforted one another after the news.

senses.definition: present participle of recomfort; comforting again
examples: They are recomforting the team after the setback.
Recomforting words helped ease anxiety.
